notes: Smoke-tested via test_worlds.sh (2026-07-22): process stayed alive 32s, no crash. Confirms process-level health but not topic content -- the original 2026-07-14 camera PASS entry never recorded an exact world_name, so this closes that gap by convention match. See notes/results/camera_tutorial.log.

Smoke-tested via test_worlds.sh with namespace:=rexrov (2026-07-22): process stayed alive 32s, no crash. First attempt without a namespace failed on the now-understood dave_robot.launch.py/robot_config.py gap -- see ocean_current_plugin.world note. See notes/results/dave_Santorini.log.

Investigated 2026-07-22: this is actually just an empty prop scene (a static 'grabbapole' + vase models) -- no robot arm/manipulator model or plugin anywhere in it, and none exists in this checkout's dave_robot_models either. The real blocker is dave_world.launch.py itself: read in full (examples/dave_demos/launch/dave_world.launch.py, 47 lines) -- it declares only world_name/verbose args, no gui/headless at all, and unconditionally includes ros_gz_sim's gz_sim.launch.py with a real GUI client (never adds the -s server-only flag the way dave_robot.launch.py/dave_sensor.launch.py do). Confirmed via real run: gz-sim-gui-client aborts ('qt.qpa.xcb: could not connect to display', SIGABRT) since this container has no X display attached to the docker exec session. Not a world-file bug -- the launch file has no headless mode, not evidence the world itself is untestable. Would need either a source patch to dave_world.launch.py (add -s when headless), an RDP/Xvfb session, or running gz sim -s <world> directly to test for real.
Same as dave_bimanual_example.world -- just prop models (M-to-F plug, electrical panel), no arm. Same dave_world.launch.py headless limitation confirmed via identical qt.qpa.xcb/SIGABRT crash trace.

UPDATED 2026-07-23: builds and produces real PointCloud2 output (513 beams x 301 rays, confirmed 2026-07-07 Mac Metal / 2026-07-11 Docker / 2026-07-20 RAM-CPU load test), and the 2026-07-22 test_worlds.sh 32s-liveness smoke test PASSed -- but that check only confirms the process doesn't crash, not that it simulates at a usable rate. Re-checked 2026-07-23 while benchmarking: on both Mac and Docker, across both wgpu/cpu compute backends and multiple fresh launches, this world either crawls at RTF ~0.012-0.015 (~80x slower than real time) or shows a simulation-progress stall for 4+ minutes (Mac: CPU climbs 100-200%+, high-CPU stall, livelock-like; Docker: CPU near-idle ~1%, near-idle stall, possible deadlock -- no thread dump/lock analysis done, symptom only). Downgraded PASS -> PARTIAL. Not root-caused at the source level. Exact command: namespace:=blueview_p900 world_name:=dave_multibeam_sonar paused:=false x:=5.8 z:=2 yaw:=3.14 compute_backend:=wgpu gui:=true headless:=true. See notes/results/dave_multibeam_sonar.log and README.md Progress Log 2026-07-23.

DISCREPANCY RESOLVED (2026-07-22): README's 2026-07-14 claim that this needs the separate IOES-Lab/dave sonar-demo branch appears to be incorrect for this checkout -- only references multibeam_sonar_system (present, built, confirmed working) plus ordinary Fuel includes. Smoke-tested: alive 31s, no crash. RE-CHECKED 2026-07-23 for the simulation-progress stall found in dave_multibeam_sonar: sampled /world/oceans_waves/stats (internal world name is 'oceans_waves', same as the base dave_ocean_waves.world, not the filename) for 20s -- sim_time was clearly advancing (41.2s to 43.6s) with RTF frequently near 0.99-1.0, unlike dave_multibeam_sonar's confirmed stall/near-zero pattern. Caveat: the sample was contaminated by a leftover duplicate gz-sim-main instance from an earlier launch attempt in the same terminal session (a second interleaved sim_time/RTF stream with much lower values was also present, real_time offset ~177s ahead) -- the low-RTF numbers in that sample are attributable to CPU contention between two concurrent instances, not necessarily this world alone. A fully clean single-instance re-sample would be needed for a fully confident number, but the clear sim_time progression and near-1.0 RTF values rule out the same catastrophic stall multibeam_sonar shows. See notes/results/dave_ocean_waves_sonar.log.

Same sonar-demo-branch resolution as dave_ocean_waves_sonar.world (2026-07-22): multibeam_sonar_system plugin confirmed present/built, no branch dependency. Smoke-tested: alive 32s, no crash. RE-CHECKED 2026-07-23 for the simulation-progress stall found in dave_multibeam_sonar: sampled /world/oceans_waves_sonar_integrated/stats cleanly (single confirmed process, no contamination) for 20s -- sim_time WAS progressing (14.93s to 15.48s over the window, not stalled/zero like dave_multibeam_sonar), but at a degraded rate, RTF fluctuating roughly 0.005-0.39 and averaging around ~0.05-0.1 (~10-20x slower than real time). Downgraded to PARTIAL: this is not the same total stall as dave_multibeam_sonar, but it is a real, confirmed performance degradation, not yet explained. Worth a longer/cleaner re-check and comparison against dave_multibeam_sonar's exact beam/ray config to see if this is the same underlying sonar-compute cost at a smaller scale. See notes/results/dave_ocean_waves_sonar_integrated.log.

Same as dave_bimanual_example.world -- just prop models (M-to-F/F-to-M plug pair), no arm. Same dave_world.launch.py headless limitation confirmed via identical qt.qpa.xcb/SIGABRT crash trace (test_worlds.sh's own PASS classification for this world was a false positive caused by a timing race -- the crash trace is present in the log despite the process still being alive at the 30s check; the classifier only flags known crash signatures if grep catches them before the alive-check point in a specific order). All 3 manipulation worlds are equally affected, not just the 2 flagged CRASH.

DVL sensor confirmed with real topic data in Progress Log (2026-07-14, 4/4 sensors -- launch and publish confirmed, not just liveness), but no world_name:= recorded in any doc at that time. Smoke-tested via test_worlds.sh (2026-07-22): process stayed alive 32s, no crash -- see notes/results/dvl_world.log.

Root cause confirmed AND fixed same day. Original bug: 'Unable to find uri[https://fuel.gazebosim.org/1.0/hmoyen/models/North-East-Down frame]' (404) -- the world file's Fuel URI used hyphens ('North-East-Down frame') where the working dave_ocean_waves.world uses spaces ('North East Down frame') for the identical model. Fixed: patched the URI to use spaces (patches/new_dvl_uri_fix.diff). Verified live in a running container, then baked into docker/lyrical.arm64v8.dockerfile as a late-layer sed fix and re-verified end-to-end on a fresh image build: 15s test window, no 404/URI error. This is liveness-level verification (SMOKE), not confirmed DVL topic data for this specific world file. See notes/results/new_dvl.log for the original failure.

Confirmed exact command (2026-07-18): dave_robot.launch.py world_name:=ocean_current_plugin. ros2 service list | grep -i current verified real /hydrodynamics/ service names -- topic/service-level verification, not just liveness. RESOLVED discrepancy (2026-07-22): a bare re-run without namespace hit '[Errno 2] No such file or directory: .../dave_robot_models/config/robot_config.py' -- traced to dave_robot.launch.py always requiring namespace to match one of dave_robot_models/config/{rexrov,bluerov2,bluerov2_heavy,bluerov2_heavy_multibeam_sonar,glider_slocum}/robot_config.py (no generic default exists). Re-run with namespace:=rexrov: alive 33s, no crash. See notes/results/ocean_current_plugin.log.

Root cause confirmed AND worked around same day. Original crash: gz sim SERVER process (-s flag) aborts (SIGABRT, exit 134) on a libstdc++ std::normal_distribution assertion failure ('_M_stddev > 0' failed) -- UsblTransponder.cc:263 constructs the distribution with sigma taken unvalidated from the world file, which set <sigma>0.0</sigma> on both UsblTransponder plugin instances. Worked around: patched sigma 0.0 -> 0.0001 in usbl_tutorial.world (patches/usbl_sigma_fix.diff) -- a world-file workaround, not a plugin-level fix. Verified live in a running container, then baked into docker/lyrical.arm64v8.dockerfile as a late-layer sed fix (cache-preserving, 16s rebuild) and re-verified end-to-end on a fresh image build from that Dockerfile: 15s test window, no assertion/abort. FUNCTIONAL PASS because the original 2026-07-14 entry already confirmed the server/plugin publishes real data (the bug was the GUI-adjacent server crash, not absent output). Plugin-level fix and upstream report not yet done -- see notes/usbl-gui-crash-investigation.md.
